trajectory.description: Observation: Fresh start, no files yet. Analysis: This is a protein fitness prediction task (regression). The key challenge is extrapolation from low-order to high-order mutants. ESM2 protein language models are pre-downloaded and perfect for this. I'll use ESM2-150M as backbone with a regression head. Strategy: 1) Extract ESM2 embeddings for the mutation region (positions 561-588) or full sequence, 2) Fine-tune with regression head. Let me start with a quick baseline: freeze ESM2, train only regression head, then submit eval to measure timing. I'll use mean pooling of ESM2 embeddings.

trajectory.description: Observation: 3 epochs with frozen ESM2 gave val_rho=0.4757. Each epoch ~630s. Analysis: Only regression head was trained (180K params). Fine-tuning ESM2 should improve significantly. Decision: Submit current checkpoint for baseline, then train with full fine-tuning using differential LR (lower for ESM2, higher for head). Also focus pooling on mutation region (positions 561-588).

trajectory.description: Observation: The eval failed because torch.load uses weights_only=True by default in PyTorch 2.6, but the checkpoint contains numpy scalars. Need to fix predict.py to use weights_only=False. Analysis: Simple fix - add weights_only=False to torch.load call. Decision: Fix predict.py and resubmit eval with the existing checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: predict.py fails because /data/test.csv doesn't exist - test data is hidden and only available during eval phase. The eval harness will pass the correct path. The local test failed because I was testing with /data which only has train/val. Analysis: The predict.py code is correct for eval - it just can't be tested locally without test.csv. The weights_only=False fix is already in place. I should submit eval with the existing checkpoint. Also need to start fine-tuning ESM2 to improve from val_rho=0.4757. Decision: Submit eval with existing checkpoint (the predict.py is correct for eval phase), then train with ESM2 fine-tuning.

Decision: Continue training for 5 more epochs from current best checkpoint to push performance higher.
